# devin_enterprise_service_user_role (Resource)

Assigns an enterprise-wide (account-level) role to an existing service user. The service user must not already have an account-level role. Use devin_org_service_user_role for organization-scoped roles. Note: the API has no way to remove an account-level role without deleting the service user itself, so destroying this resource only removes it from Terraform state and leaves the assignment in place.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "devin_roles" "all" {}
data "devin_service_users" "all" {}

locals {
  enterprise_admin_role_id = one([
    for r in data.devin_roles.all.roles : r.role_id
    if r.role_type == "enterprise" && r.role_name == "Admin"
  ])
  ci_service_user_id = one([
    for su in data.devin_service_users.all.service_users : su.service_user_id
    if su.name == "CI Pipeline"
  ])
}

# Grant an existing service user an enterprise-wide role. Destroying this
# resource leaves the assignment in place (see the resource documentation).
resource "devin_enterprise_service_user_role" "ci" {
  service_user_id = local.ci_service_user_id
  role_id         = local.enterprise_admin_role_id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `role_id` (String) Role to assign. Must be an enterprise (account-level) role.
- `service_user_id` (String) ID of an existing service user.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Enterprise service user roles are imported with the service user ID.
terraform import devin_enterprise_service_user_role.ci su-abc123
```
